name: entry_size_decreases_with_depth
reason: Higher layers have larger-or-equal entry sizes — direct consequence of the verified lemma, tests that the invariant enforces monotonicity

name: adjacent_entry_size_geq
reason: Adjacent layers satisfy monotonicity — the base case of the induction

name: entry_size_equal_when_num_entries_one
code:
proof fn phi_3_entry_size_equal_when_num_entries_one(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
        arch.num_entries(i + 1) == 1,
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) == arch.entry_size(i + 1),
{
}
reason: When a layer has exactly 1 entry, parent and child entry sizes are equal — the invariant allows `num_entries == 1` which means a layer maps the same size as its child, creating a redundant nesting level

name: inv_allows_non_power_of_two_entries
reason: The invariant permits non-power-of-two entry sizes and num_entries — x86 page tables always use powers of two, but `inv()` allows arbitrary factorizations like 6 = 2 * 3

name: inv_single_layer_any_entry_size
reason: A single-layer architecture with arbitrary entry_size satisfies `inv()` — `entry_size_is_next_layer_size` is vacuously true for the only layer, so the sole constraint is `0 < entry_size <= X86_MAX_ENTRY_SIZE`
